topic: Sharp non-equivalence and strict-extension theorem between three leading continuous-treatment difference-in-differences estimators for the Average Causal Response (ACR) curve: DHS quantile-matching continuous-treatment DiD, de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille generalized parallel trends/local-linear continuous-dose DiD, and panel TWFE-with-continuous-treatment.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp equality frontier among DHS quantile-matching ACR, de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille switcher/local-linear ACR, and continuous-dose TWFE projection limits.

source_refs.claim: Identifies continuous-treatment causal effects from a data-defined control group using crossing treatment CDFs, stationarity of unobservables, and rank invariance of time trends.
source_refs.author: D'Haultfoeuille, Hoderlein, Sasaki

source_refs.claim: Identifies average slopes of switchers' potential outcomes by comparing switchers and stayers with the same baseline treatment under parallel trends.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille, Pasquier, Sow, Vazquez-Bare

source_refs.excerpt: The four named implementations have a common influence function if and only if this rank-zero condition holds
upgrade Conjecture 1 from population-functional equality to an estimator-level iff frontier

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published identification arguments use different primitives: DHS relies on quantile/rank matching across treatment distributions, the dCDH line uses switcher-stayer or local dose comparisons in panels, and TWFE is a residualized linear projection. Prior ThmSmith attempts found adjacent rank-zero equality certificates but left estimator-level iff frontiers conjectural, so the missing object is the exact intersection of rank/CDF, support, local trend, and projection-weight restrictions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient equivalence theorem for the three ACR(d) identifying functionals, with strict counterexamples when any equality-frontier condition fails.

open_problems.open_problem: Classify when continuous-dose TWFE residualized weights collapse to a causal ACR derivative and when they produce a strict signed-weight or composition-contaminated limit.

source_refs.claim: Continuous-treatment TWFE parameters can be hard to interpret even with two periods, while generalized parallel trends identifies treated-type parameters but not clean dose comparisons without stronger assumptions.
source_refs.author: Callaway, Goodman-Bacon, Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: Empirical researchers often use TWFE for continuous treatments, but those estimators are not generally robust to heterogeneous treatment effects and rely on linearity.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille, Vazquez-Bare

source_refs.claim: TWFE with staggered treatment timing is a weighted average of all possible two-group/two-period DiD estimators, motivating signed-weight decompositions.
source_refs.author: Goodman-Bacon

source_refs.excerpt: every inclusion in Theorem is strict on a nonempty open set
has no residual row c_method-a_att, so R <= R_star cannot imply equality

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature separates continuous-dose selection/target-parameter problems from TWFE weighting problems, and the prior strict-lattice proposal was rejected because it lacked a residual row tying the method coefficient to the target estimand. For continuous ACR(d), the unresolved obstacle is an explicit residualized dose-weight decomposition whose zero rows exactly match causal derivative equality rather than merely span inclusion.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A signed-weight decomposition theorem for continuous-treatment TWFE, giving zero-bias iff restrictions and open-set strict-separation witnesses relative to DHS and dCDH ACR functionals.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine when local pre-period, bandwidth, or smooth-trend restrictions are redundant diagnostics versus score-changing constraints for continuous-treatment ACR(d) identification.

source_refs.claim: Develops robust inference for DiD and event-study designs when parallel trends may be violated within structured deviation classes.
source_refs.author: Rambachan, Roth

source_refs.claim: Uses pre-event observables to construct counterfactual trends in panel event-study designs, adding structure when simple parallel trends is suspect.
source_refs.author: Freyaldenhoven, Hansen, Shapiro

source_refs.excerpt: equality is now classified by the full two-block certificate K_e=(D_e;H_{pre,e})
M_star at which CI(M) first crosses zero

open_problems.why_unsolved: Honest-DiD and proxy/pretrend papers give binary/event-time sensitivity tools, while continuous-treatment ACR estimands add local dose bandwidths, derivative targets, and support-dependent extrapolation. Prior proposals isolated pre-period residual certificates but not the dose-local bandwidth operator that decides whether the restriction leaves the ACR score unchanged.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A projection or support-function theorem giving the dose-bandwidth/trend subspace whose residual is zero exactly when the ACR(d) score is unchanged, plus a sharp breakdown frontier when it is nonzero.

open_problems.open_problem: Resolve when continuous-dose rank-slice slopes, average derivatives, and DiD dose-change functionals define the same ACR(d) object rather than distinct derivative targets.

source_refs.excerpt: sharp finite LP envelope for adjacent-rank dose-response slope
ADE sensitivity bounds do not answer the baseline-rank-slice adjacent finite-difference sign question
density-version obstruction collapses the only field-tier kernel

open_problems.why_unsolved: The mined dose-response entries solve or frame narrow finite/rank-slice slope questions, but they also expose density-version and joint-pasting obstacles. For continuous-treatment DiD, the unresolved issue is whether exact-dose local limits, average derivative conventions, and baseline-rank finite differences are interchangeable under the assumptions used by DHS or dCDH.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A target-equivalence theorem identifying ACR(d) as a local dose-change functional under explicit density and regular-conditional conventions, with separation examples for rank-slice, average-derivative, and DiD targets.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive sharp partial-identification bounds for the ACR(d) curve when continuous-treatment DiD lacks exact stayers and near-zero-dose controls.

source_refs.claim: Develops continuous-treatment DiD without stayers and extends nonparametric results while adding parametric approaches for limitations of the nonparametric design.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille, Vazquez-Bare

source_refs.claim: For heterogeneous adoption without stayers, proposes bandwidth-based near-zero-dose controls if available, but bounds or parametric restrictions when they are not.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin, Ciccia, D'Haultfoeuille, Knau

open_problems.why_unsolved: The literature distinguishes point identification with exact stayers or near-zero controls from bounds or parametric extrapolation without them, but it does not supply the sharp identified set for the whole ACR curve under minimal smoothness and support-gap restrictions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp-bound theorem for ACR(d) indexed by support gaps and dose-bandwidth limits, with constructive least-favorable potential-outcome distributions.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize equivalence and contamination for dynamic or staggered continuous-treatment panels where current and lagged dose effects both enter ACR(d).

source_refs.claim: Proposes event-study estimators for effects of exposure to a weakly higher treatment dose over multiple periods and notes TWFE bias with heterogeneous effects.
source_refs.author: de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille

source_refs.claim: Event-study leads and lags can be contaminated by treatment effects from other periods under heterogeneous dynamic effects.
source_refs.author: Sun, Abraham

open_problems.why_unsolved: Continuous-dose ACR estimands are local in dose, while dynamic DiD estimands are local in event time and exposure history. The missing regime statement must align dose-path support, lag restrictions, and cohort timing before DHS-style marginal effects, dCDH local-linear estimands, and TWFE event-study coefficients can be compared.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An identification formula for dynamic ACR_lag(d) and an equivalence theorem specifying when local-linear cohort-dose estimators, DHS-style marginal effects, and TWFE event-study coefficients coincide.

literature_map:
Athey and Imbens (2006, Econometrica) provide the nonlinear/change-in-changes foundation: distributional counterfactuals replace additive parallel trends.
D'Haultfoeuille, Hoderlein, and Sasaki (2023, Journal of Econometrics) move this logic to continuous treatments using CDF crossing, rank invariance, and a data-defined control group.
de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille, Pasquier, Sow, and Vazquez-Bare (2022/2024 working paper line) study panel settings where treatment is continuous every period and identify switcher slopes using stayers with the same baseline treatment.
de Chaisemartin, D'Haultfoeuille, and Vazquez-Bare (2024, AEA P&P) target continuous-treatment designs without stayers and explicitly position TWFE as common but nonrobust under heterogeneity and nonlinearity.
de Chaisemartin, Ciccia, D'Haultfoeuille, and Knau (2024/2025 CREST) study heterogeneous adoption without true controls, using near-zero-dose bandwidth controls, bounds, or parametric extrapolation.
Callaway, Goodman-Bacon, and Sant'Anna (2024, NBER) frame continuous-treatment DiD around treated-type parameters, selection across doses, and decompositions showing TWFE difficulty even in two periods.
Goodman-Bacon (2021, Journal of Econometrics) supplies the canonical TWFE weighted-average decomposition for staggered binary timing, a template for continuous-dose decompositions.
de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020/2023 survey and AER/J Econometrics work) document misleading TWFE behavior with heterogeneous effects and motivate robust alternatives.
Sun and Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics) show event-study coefficients can be contaminated across leads/lags under heterogeneous dynamic effects.
de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2022/2024 intertemporal treatment effects) extend DiD/event-study logic to weakly higher treatment doses over exposure horizons.
Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, Review of Economic Studies) give an imputation framework and efficiency benchmark for robust event-study estimation.
Rambachan and Roth (2023, Review of Economic Studies) shift exact parallel trends toward honest sensitivity analysis, relevant for local dose-trend violations.
Freyaldenhoven, Hansen, and Shapiro (2019, AER) use pre-event covariates/proxies to discipline counterfactual trends when simple parallel trends is suspect.
de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2023, Journal of Econometrics, several treatments) show multi-treatment TWFE contamination, a direct concern for multi-dose ACR curves.
The unresolved tension is that continuous-treatment DiD has several plausible estimands, but the literature has not yet drawn a sharp equivalence boundary among quantile-matching ACR, switcher-slope/local-dose ACR, and TWFE projection limits.
prior_proposal_map:
Mined the active eid_continuous_did_acr_panel state/pipeline; it has only initialization/state signal and no proposal yet.
High signal came from eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1: rank-zero EIF/non-equivalence conjectures, §11 scope flags, and banking notes.
High signal came from panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: strict span-lattice and reviewer requests to upgrade population equality to estimator-level iff frontiers.
pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1 yielded a sharp support-function interval and zero-bound DCDH/Callaway-Sant'Anna recovery adjacent to DiD sensitivity.
pid_dose_response_iv_v1 and pid_dose_response_shape_v1 yielded continuous-dose derivative, rank-slice, density-version, and pasting obstacles.
panel_honest_did_phase_v1 yielded bandwidth/sensitivity-threshold geometry but was flagged as calibration rather than flagship estimator non-equivalence.
